perturbation.original_text: Definitions 1.1 In this Agreement, including the recitals, except as expressly provided or unless the context otherwise requires, (a) Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party or vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person, (b) Area of Non-Competition means the State of Zacatecas, Mexico and the area extending 20 kilometers in all directions from the external boundary of each mineral property owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest, in Mexico, as at the Effective Date, (c) Business Day means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or a civic or statutory holiday in Reno, Nevada and Vancouver, British Columbia, (d) Closing means the completion of the transactions contemplated by the Arrangement Agreement, (e) Designated Mineral Property means a mineral property or individual mineral concession within a mineral property, that is owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest in Mexico as of the Effective Date, (f) Effective Date means May 3, 200ri or such later date as determined under the Arrangement Agreement, (g) Mining Activities means any acquisition of mineral rights or any mineral exploration or development activities, in any manner whatsoever, and (h) Person means an individual, corporation, body corporate, firm, limited liability company, parmership, syndicate, joint venture, society, association, trust or unincorporated organization.
perturbation.changed_text: Definitions 1.1 In this Agreement, including the recitals, except as expressly provided or unless the context otherwise requires, (a) Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party or vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person. For the purposes of Section 2.1, an Affiliate shall only mean a corporation where the party owns more than 50% of the voting shares. (b) Area of Non-Competition means the State of Zacatecas, Mexico and the area extending 20 kilometers in all directions from the external boundary of each mineral property owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest, in Mexico, as at the Effective Date, (c) Business Day means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or a civic or statutory holiday in Reno, Nevada and Vancouver, British Columbia, (d) Closing means the completion of the transactions contemplated by the Arrangement Agreement, (e) Designated Mineral Property means a mineral property or individual mineral concession within a mineral property, that is owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest in Mexico as of the Effective Date, (f) Effective Date means May 3, 200ri or such later date as determined under the Arrangement Agreement, (g) Mining Activities means any acquisition of mineral rights or any mineral exploration or development activities, in any manner whatsoever, and (h) Person means an individual, corporation, body corporate, firm, limited liability company, parmership, syndicate, joint venture, society, association, trust or unincorporated organization.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by redefining 'Affiliate' for the specific purposes of Section 2.1, limiting its scope to entities where the party owns more than 50% of the voting shares. This contradicts the original, broader definition of 'Affiliate' provided in Section 1.1(a). This means that the non-compete clause in section 2.1 will only apply to affiliates where there is ownership of more than 50% of the voting shares, leading to potential legal loopholes if an affiliate is controlled through other means, such as contractual agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Western Copper covenants and agrees with Glamis that, for a period of 2 years after the Effective Date, it will not, directly or indirectly, either individually or in partnership or jointly or in conjunction with any Person, which will include being a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor of such Person or in any other manner whatsoever, (a) carry on or be engaged in Mining Activities, or i 162967.3 -4- (b) advise, lend money to, guarantee the debts or obligations of or permit its name to be used by any Person who carries on or is engaged in Mining Activities, in the Area of Non-Competition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a narrower definition of 'Affiliate' specifically for Section 2.1, requiring more than 50% ownership of voting shares. This contradicts the original, broader definition in Section 1.1(a), which covers control by any means, not just share ownership. The contradicted text in Section 2.1 states that Western Copper covenants not to engage in mining activities 'in conjunction with any Person, which will include being a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor of such Person or in any other manner whatsoever'. By narrowing the definition of Affiliate, the revised text creates a loophole where Western Copper could engage in mining activities with a person or entity controlled by them through means other than 50% share ownership, potentially circumventing the non-compete agreement. This is a direct contradiction because the original intent was to prevent any form of Mining Activities with controlled entities, but the change allows Mining Activities with entities controlled in other ways (e.g., contractual agreement) when the Contradicted text Section 2.1 refers to affiliates.

perturbation.original_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment, it will give Western Copper notice (the ' Disposition Notice") of the intended disposition. For a period of 30 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the ease of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 30 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.changed_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis may, at its discretion, choose to give Western Copper notice (the 'Disposition Notice') of its intended disposition of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment. If Glamis provides a Disposition Notice, then for a period of 30 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 30 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.explanation: The change alters the Right of First Offer clause by changing 'Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of...' to 'Glamis may, at its discretion, choose to give Western Copper notice...'. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts the original intent of a guaranteed Right of First Offer. Now, giving Western Copper the Disposition Notice is not mandatory. This undermines the initial agreement where Western Copper was guaranteed the right to make an offer when Glamis decided to dispose of its Designated Mineral Property. This creates an in-text contradiction if other clauses in the agreement assume that Western Copper will always receive a disposition notice. The contradiction occurs as Section 2.2 originally guarantees Western Copper a chance to make an offer before Glamis disposes of a property. Now, this is not the case.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis may, at its discretion, choose to give Western Copper notice (the 'Disposition Notice') of its intended disposition of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment. If Glamis provides a Disposition Notice, then for a period of 30 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 30 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Glamis 'covenants and agrees' to provide notice, indicating a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'Glamis may, at its discretion, choose to give notice,' making the notice optional. This directly contradicts the implied guarantee in the rest of Section 2.2 that Western Copper will receive a disposition notice, thus creating a direct contradiction, making the answer 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Glamis' Remedies for Breach 3.1 Western Copper acknowledges and agrees that a breach of its covenants contained in this Agreement would result in damage to Glamis that could not adequately be compensated for by monetary award alone, Accordingly, Western Copper agrees that in the event of any such breach, in addition to any other remedies available to Glamis at law or otherwise, Glamis will be entitled, as a matter of right and without the need to prove damage, to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for relief by way of injunction, restraining order, decree or otherwise as may be appropriate to ensure compliance by Western Copper with the provisions of this Agreement and to restrain any breach of this Agreement by Western Copper, Any remedy expressly set forth in this §3.1 wall be in addition to and not inclusive of or dependent upon the exercise of any other remedy available to Glamis at law or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Glamis' Remedies for Breach 3.1 Western Copper acknowledges and agrees that a breach of its covenants contained in this Agreement *may* result in damage to Glamis that could not adequately be compensated for by monetary award alone, Accordingly, Western Copper agrees that in the event of any such breach, in addition to any other remedies available to Glamis at law or otherwise, Glamis *may* be entitled to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for relief by way of injunction, restraining order, decree or otherwise as may be appropriate to ensure compliance by Western Copper with the provisions of this Agreement and to restrain any breach of this Agreement by Western Copper, Any remedy expressly set forth in this §3.1 wall be in addition to and not inclusive of or dependent upon the exercise of any other remedy available to Glamis at law or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'would result' to 'may result' and 'will be entitled' to 'may be entitled', the certainty of Glamis' remedies for breach is undermined. This introduces ambiguity and creates an in-text contradiction to the general principle of the agreement (explicitly stated in 3.3) that all restrictions are reasonable and valid. Previously, a breach automatically entitled Glamis to seek injunctive relief. Now, the entitlement is discretionary, making enforcement uncertain. Further, in the event Glamis, in its option, chooses to give up the right of remedies for breach, it creates contradiction with section 3.3 in which all defences to the strict enforcement thereof by Western Copper or Glamis, as the case may be, arc hereby waived by them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of Western Copper and Glamis agree that all restrictions in this Agreement applicable to them are reasonable and valid, and all defences to the strict enforcement thereof by Western Copper or Glamis, as the case may be, arc hereby waived by them.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'would result' with 'may result' and 'will be entitled' with 'may be entitled', introducing uncertainty regarding Glamis's remedies for breach. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'all restrictions in this Agreement applicable to them are reasonable and valid, and all defenses to the strict enforcement thereof...are hereby waived'. The original text guaranteed Glamis injunctive relief in case of breach, while the changed text makes it discretionary, creating an ambiguity regarding enforcement of the agreement and invalidating the waiver of defenses to strict enforcement. This change creates an in-text contradiction by undermining the certainty and enforceability of the remedies.
